AMPK.definition: Adenosine monophosphate-activated protein kinase; a key enzyme regulating cellular energy homeostasis, activating cell cleanup processes.

mTOR.definition: Mechanistic target of rapamycin; a protein kinase that acts as a central regulator of cell growth, proliferation, and protein synthesis.

autophagy.definition: The natural, regulated mechanism of the cell that removes unnecessary or dysfunctional components, recycling cellular materials.

metabolic flexibility.definition: The capacity for the body to switch adaptively between substrate sources (carbohydrates and lipids) depending on physiological demands.

circadian rhythm.definition: The internal 24-hour biological clock regulating sleep, hormone release, and metabolic scheduling.

melatonin.definition: A hormone produced by the pineal gland that plays a key role in signaling nightfall and regulating sleep-wake cycles.

adenosine.definition: A cellular neuromodulator that accumulates during waking hours, generating homeostatic sleep pressure.

healthspan.definition: The period of life spent free from chronic disease, disability, and functional decline.

metabolic syndrome.definition: A cluster of metabolic dysregulations (insulin resistance, high blood pressure, abdominal obesity) that elevate cardiovascular disease risk.

cardiorespiratory fitness.definition: The capacity of the respiratory and circulatory systems to supply oxygen to working muscles during sustained exertion (often measured via VO2 max).

GRADE.definition: Grading of Recommendations, Assessment, Development, and Evaluation; a standardized framework for rating the quality of scientific evidence from High to Very Low.

Evidence Grade.definition: A standardized assessment based on the GRADE framework to rate the quality of research supporting an entry, ranging from High to Very Low.
